{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Account Officer",
  "description": "Proforce Intelligence Systems is a leading cybersecurity company that provids advanced software and hardware intelligence solutions. We empower governments, enterprises, and security agencies with cutting-edge technologies and analytical capabilities that transform complex data into actionable insights. Our mission is to help organizations stay ahead of emerging threats and opportunities through secure, scalable, and innovative solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ode Remo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and reliable Account Officer to support the finance team in managing financial records, monitoring transactions, manage payroll, and ensuring accuracy in financial reporting.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate financial records and accounting documentation
	Prepare invoices, receipts, and payment vouchers.
	Perform reconciliation of accounts, including bank and ledger reconciliations
	Payroll computation
	Monitor company expenses and income.
	Prepare financial reports and assist in budgeting.
	Ensure proper documentation of financial transactions
	Ensure compliance with financial policies and procedures
	Support internal and external audit processes when required


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field with&nbsp;2&ndash;3 years of experience in an accounting role
	Strong knowledge of account reconciliation and financial reporting
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail
	Good analytical and organizational skills.
	Strong knowledge of payroll management


Salary
N200,000 &ndash; N250,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to:&nbsp;Hr@proforceintelligence.com&nbsp;Cc:&nbsp;marvelous@proforceintelligence.com&nbsp;using &quot;Account Officer&quot; as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-03-24",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Proforce Intelligence Systems Limited"
    
  },

      "addressCountry": "NG"

Account Officer Job at Proforce Intelligence Systems Limited




        
    

        
        Proforce Intelligence Systems is a leading cybersecurity company that provids advanced software and hardware intelligence solutions. We empower governments, enterprises, and security agencies with cutting-edge technologies and analytical capabilities that transform complex data into actionable insights. Our mission is to help organizations stay ahead of emerging threats and opportunities through secure, scalable, and innovative solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Officer

Job Location: Ode Remo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a detail-oriented and reliable Account Officer to support the finance team in managing financial records, monitoring transactions, manage payroll, and ensuring accuracy in financial reporting.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate financial records and accounting documentation
	Prepare invoices, receipts, and payment vouchers.
	Perform reconciliation of accounts, including bank and ledger reconciliations
	Payroll computation
	Monitor company expenses and income.
	Prepare financial reports and assist in budgeting.
	Ensure proper documentation of financial transactions
	Ensure compliance with financial policies and procedures
	Support internal and external audit processes when required


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field with 2–3 years of experience in an accounting role
	Strong knowledge of account reconciliation and financial reporting
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail
	Good analytical and organizational skills.
	Strong knowledge of payroll management


Salary
N200,000 – N250,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: Hr@proforceintelligence.com Cc: marvelous@proforceintelligence.com using "Account Officer" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Laboratory Scientist",
  "description": "Acouns Laboratory and Diagnostic Centre is a molecular diagnostic centre in Ajah, Lekki, Lagos State, Nigeria aimed at providing quality and dependable patient test results in order to improve patient outcomes and quality of life.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Laboratory Scientist

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	The Laboratory Scientist is responsible for conducting accurate, reliable, and timely laboratory testing at Acouns Laboratory.
	This role ensures strict adherence to standard operating procedures (SOPs), quality control measures, and regulatory standards.
	The Laboratory Scientist performs testing, validates results, and contributes to overall laboratory efficiency and client satisfaction.
	The role also supports continuous improvement initiatives, maintains equipment and reagent integrity, and works collaboratively with the laboratory team to ensure excellent service delivery.


Core Functional Pillars:


	Laboratory Testing &amp; Sample Handling
	Quality Control &amp; Assurance
	Regulatory Compliance &amp; Safety
	Equipment Operation &amp; Maintenance
	Record Keeping &amp; Documentation
	Client Service &amp; Result Accuracy
	Continuous Learning &amp; Professional Development


Key Responsiblities
Laboratory Testing &amp; Sample Handling:


	Receive, label, and process laboratory samples accurately and in a timely manner.
	Perform routine and specialized laboratory tests following SOPs and standard protocols.
	Ensure proper sample storage, preservation, and handling to maintain integrity.
	Participate in multi-step laboratory workflows and assist in troubleshooting testing issues.


Quality Control &amp; Assurance:


	Conduct internal quality control (IQC) tests and participate in external quality assurance (EQA) programs.
	Ensure all test results are accurate, precise, and reproducible.
	Report any inconsistencies or deviations to the Laboratory Manager promptly.
	Follow laboratory best practices to maintain high-quality standards.


Regulatory Compliance &amp; Safety:


	Comply with regulatory standards, laboratory policies, and professional ethics.
	Handle biological samples, chemicals, and reagents safely following biosafety guidelines.
	Maintain patient confidentiality and data protection at all times.
	Adhere to all statutory and accreditation requirements.


Equipment Operation &amp; Maintenance:


	Operate laboratory instruments and equipment correctly and efficiently.
	Perform routine calibration, cleaning, and maintenance as per manufacturer guidelines.
	Report malfunctioning or faulty equipment promptly.
	Support inventory checks and reagent management to avoid stock-outs.


Record Keeping &amp; Documentation:


	Maintain accurate test logs, QC data, and laboratory records.
	Document all laboratory procedures and results as required for audits and accreditation.
	Prepare reports for the Laboratory Manager when necessary.


Client Service &amp; Result Accuracy:


	Ensure timely delivery of test results within agreed turnaround times (TAT).
	Review test results for accuracy before submission or release.
	Respond professionally to queries or concerns from clients or healthcare providers.


Continuous Learning &amp; Professional Development:


	Participate in training sessions, workshops, and professional development activities.
	Stay updated on new laboratory techniques, instruments, and regulatory guidelines.
	Support mentoring and coaching of junior laboratory staff when required.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Medical Laboratory Science or related field
	Valid MLSCN practicing license
	2&ndash;5 years of laboratory experience
	Knowledge of diagnostic laboratory operations and compliance frameworks
	Experience with quality control systems, audits, or EQA participation is an advantage.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong laboratory technical expertise and analytical skills
	Meticulous attention to detail and accuracy in testing
	Good understanding of quality control, quality assurance, and laboratory SOPs
	Knowledge of laboratory safety, ethics, and regulatory standards
	Proficiency in operating laboratory equipment and instruments
	Strong documentation, reporting, and record-keeping abilities
	Teamwork, collaboration, and professional communication skills


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 per month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter&nbsp;to:&nbsp;workforce.sm@247kpg.com&nbsp;using &ldquo;Laboratory Scientist&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-07",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-04",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Acouns Laboratory and Diagnostic Centre"
    
  },

Laboratory Scientist Job at Acouns Laboratory and Diagnostic Centre




        
    

        
        Acouns Laboratory and Diagnostic Centre is a molecular diagnostic centre in Ajah, Lekki, Lagos State, Nigeria aimed at providing quality and dependable patient test results in order to improve patient outcomes and quality of life.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Laboratory Scientist

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Laboratory Scientist is responsible for conducting accurate, reliable, and timely laboratory testing at Acouns Laboratory.
	This role ensures strict adherence to standard operating procedures (SOPs), quality control measures, and regulatory standards.
	The Laboratory Scientist performs testing, validates results, and contributes to overall laboratory efficiency and client satisfaction.
	The role also supports continuous improvement initiatives, maintains equipment and reagent integrity, and works collaboratively with the laboratory team to ensure excellent service delivery.


Core Functional Pillars:


	Laboratory Testing & Sample Handling
	Quality Control & Assurance
	Regulatory Compliance & Safety
	Equipment Operation & Maintenance
	Record Keeping & Documentation
	Client Service & Result Accuracy
	Continuous Learning & Professional Development


Key Responsiblities
Laboratory Testing & Sample Handling:


	Receive, label, and process laboratory samples accurately and in a timely manner.
	Perform routine and specialized laboratory tests following SOPs and standard protocols.
	Ensure proper sample storage, preservation, and handling to maintain integrity.
	Participate in multi-step laboratory workflows and assist in troubleshooting testing issues.


Quality Control & Assurance:


	Conduct internal quality control (IQC) tests and participate in external quality assurance (EQA) programs.
	Ensure all test results are accurate, precise, and reproducible.
	Report any inconsistencies or deviations to the Laboratory Manager promptly.
	Follow laboratory best practices to maintain high-quality standards.


Regulatory Compliance & Safety:


	Comply with regulatory standards, laboratory policies, and professional ethics.
	Handle biological samples, chemicals, and reagents safely following biosafety guidelines.
	Maintain patient confidentiality and data protection at all times.
	Adhere to all statutory and accreditation requirements.


Equipment Operation & Maintenance:


	Operate laboratory instruments and equipment correctly and efficiently.
	Perform routine calibration, cleaning, and maintenance as per manufacturer guidelines.
	Report malfunctioning or faulty equipment promptly.
	Support inventory checks and reagent management to avoid stock-outs.


Record Keeping & Documentation:


	Maintain accurate test logs, QC data, and laboratory records.
	Document all laboratory procedures and results as required for audits and accreditation.
	Prepare reports for the Laboratory Manager when necessary.


Client Service & Result Accuracy:


	Ensure timely delivery of test results within agreed turnaround times (TAT).
	Review test results for accuracy before submission or release.
	Respond professionally to queries or concerns from clients or healthcare providers.


Continuous Learning & Professional Development:


	Participate in training sessions, workshops, and professional development activities.
	Stay updated on new laboratory techniques, instruments, and regulatory guidelines.
	Support mentoring and coaching of junior laboratory staff when required.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Medical Laboratory Science or related field
	Valid MLSCN practicing license
	2–5 years of laboratory experience
	Knowledge of diagnostic laboratory operations and compliance frameworks
	Experience with quality control systems, audits, or EQA participation is an advantage.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong laboratory technical expertise and analytical skills
	Meticulous attention to detail and accuracy in testing
	Good understanding of quality control, quality assurance, and laboratory SOPs
	Knowledge of laboratory safety, ethics, and regulatory standards
	Proficiency in operating laboratory equipment and instruments
	Strong documentation, reporting, and record-keeping abilities
	Teamwork, collaboration, and professional communication skills


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 per month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: workforce.sm@247kpg.com using “Laboratory Scientist” as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Transport / Facility Officer",
  "description": "Daily Need Distributors Limited is a leading FMCG distributing company in West Africa covering 4 states in North Central Nigeria, with Head Office in Abuja, Nigeria. We have been delivering excellent values for over 26 years of existence. Our experience span through result driven partnerships with multinational, intercontinental and local organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Transport / Facility Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Idu, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
Transport:


	Ensure all Drivers and Mechanics fill the log book regularly and ensure all organization&rsquo;s Drivers have valid Driver&#39;s license and clean driving record.
	Coordinate with all Heads of Departments and Units to determine transport requirement and schedule transport to meet their needs and coordinate the movement of vehicles.
	Ensure appropriate use, maintenance and repair of all vehicles and trucks (private or leased), motorcycles and generators and keep accurate documentation of all vehicle document such as vehicle registration, insurance, vehicle logs, daily inspection reports, speed limits, etc.
	Act as Liaison Officer between the organization and local government agencies and personnel necessary to keep the organization vehicle fleet legal and road worthy.
	Maintain proper inventory of all components of vehicles, making sure they are maintained and inspected frequently and ensure Drivers have an updated license.
	Oversee recruitment of new Drivers and conduct driving test in traffic rules and signs.
	Act as line manager to all Drivers, evaluate Drivers performance, allocate and rotate appropriate work assignments and develop training programs for all Drivers.
	Ensure all vehicles are used in accordance with FRSC rules and regulations and that vehicle usage data is correctly recorded and perform any other related duties when required.
	Adhere strictly to company&#39;s policies including the Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).


Facility:


	Oversee the day-to-day operation of all facility services to ensure that utilities and infrastructure function efficiently and without interruption.
	Conduct routine inspections of company premises to identify maintenance needs, safety risks, and environmental concerns, ensuring a clean, safe, and well-maintained workplace.
	Manage and ensure proper maintenance, servicing, and protection of company assets, including vehicles, buildings, office equipment, and other operational resources.
	Coordinate and supervise facility-related works such as repairs, installations, and preventive maintenance activities by external vendors and internal technical staff.
	Respond promptly to unexpected facility breakdowns or operational disruptions, ensuring timely repair, recovery, and minimal impact on business activities.
	Monitor and enforce preventive maintenance schedules for critical equipment such as generators, air conditioning systems, and other essential facility infrastructure.
	Liaise and negotiate with relevant government agencies and regulatory bodies on matters relating to facility compliance, permits, inspections, and operational requirements.
	Carry out other responsibilities assigned to you by the General Manager or Managing Director.


Requirements


	Must have a minimum qualification of BSc / HND
	Minimum of 3 years of experience.
	Must be matured, responsible and well-mannered.
	Ability to read and write.
	Possession of a valid driver&#39;s license and not a learner&#39;s permit.
	Ability to drive manual vehicles.
	Preferably a male.


Benefits


	Starting salary: N175,000 (To be reviewed upon confirmation).
	Annual leave allowance.
	Health insurance cover.
	Pension scheme.
	Career development programs.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@dndng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Transport / Facility Officer Job at Daily Need Distributors Limited




        
    

        
        Daily Need Distributors Limited is a leading FMCG distributing company in West Africa covering 4 states in North Central Nigeria, with Head Office in Abuja, Nigeria. We have been delivering excellent values for over 26 years of existence. Our experience span through result driven partnerships with multinational, intercontinental and local organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Transport / Facility Officer

Job Location: Idu, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
Transport:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Ensure all Drivers and Mechanics fill the log book regularly and ensure all organization’s Drivers have valid Driver's license and clean driving record.
	Coordinate with all Heads of Departments and Units to determine transport requirement and schedule transport to meet their needs and coordinate the movement of vehicles.
	Ensure appropriate use, maintenance and repair of all vehicles and trucks (private or leased), motorcycles and generators and keep accurate documentation of all vehicle document such as vehicle registration, insurance, vehicle logs, daily inspection reports, speed limits, etc.
	Act as Liaison Officer between the organization and local government agencies and personnel necessary to keep the organization vehicle fleet legal and road worthy.
	Maintain proper inventory of all components of vehicles, making sure they are maintained and inspected frequently and ensure Drivers have an updated license.
	Oversee recruitment of new Drivers and conduct driving test in traffic rules and signs.
	Act as line manager to all Drivers, evaluate Drivers performance, allocate and rotate appropriate work assignments and develop training programs for all Drivers.
	Ensure all vehicles are used in accordance with FRSC rules and regulations and that vehicle usage data is correctly recorded and perform any other related duties when required.
	Adhere strictly to company's policies including the Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).


Facility:


	Oversee the day-to-day operation of all facility services to ensure that utilities and infrastructure function efficiently and without interruption.
	Conduct routine inspections of company premises to identify maintenance needs, safety risks, and environmental concerns, ensuring a clean, safe, and well-maintained workplace.
	Manage and ensure proper maintenance, servicing, and protection of company assets, including vehicles, buildings, office equipment, and other operational resources.
	Coordinate and supervise facility-related works such as repairs, installations, and preventive maintenance activities by external vendors and internal technical staff.
	Respond promptly to unexpected facility breakdowns or operational disruptions, ensuring timely repair, recovery, and minimal impact on business activities.
	Monitor and enforce preventive maintenance schedules for critical equipment such as generators, air conditioning systems, and other essential facility infrastructure.
	Liaise and negotiate with relevant government agencies and regulatory bodies on matters relating to facility compliance, permits, inspections, and operational requirements.
	Carry out other responsibilities assigned to you by the General Manager or Managing Director.


Requirements


	Must have a minimum qualification of BSc / HND
	Minimum of 3 years of experience.
	Must be matured, responsible and well-mannered.
	Ability to read and write.
	Possession of a valid driver's license and not a learner's permit.
	Ability to drive manual vehicles.
	Preferably a male.


Benefits


	Starting salary: N175,000 (To be reviewed upon confirmation).
	Annual leave allowance.
	Health insurance cover.
	Pension scheme.
	Career development programs.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Executive",
  "description": "TheHRHive - Our client, a Financial Services firm, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	The Senior Executive is a pivotal role for an experienced professional with unmatched expertise in the microfinance banking sector.
	This individual will drive high-level sales strategy, lead market expansion efforts, and play a key role in shaping the bank&rsquo;s growth trajectory.
	The ideal candidate combines a visionary mindset with a structured, data-driven approach,bringing exceptional organisational skills and strategic insight to every facet of the role.
	Beyond achieving sales objectives, this leader will contribute significantly to building operational systems and fostering a high-performance culture, positioning Aella for long-term success in the dynamic financial ecosystem.


Key Responsibilities
Strategic Leadership:


	Architect and execute sales strategies that align with the bank&rsquo;s vision for market leadership and revenue growth.
	Analyse market trends, customer behaviour, and regulatory developments to inform proactive decision-making.
	Partner with executive leadership to integrate sales objectives with overall organisational goals, offering strategic input on business priorities.


Market Expansion and Business Growth:


	Identify and capitalise on emerging opportunities in untapped markets, ensuring sustainable growth.
	Build relationships with key stakeholders across the microfinance sector, enhancing the bank&rsquo;s visibility and influence.
	Lead the introduction and scaling of innovative financial solutions tailored to diverse customer needs.


Operational Excellence:


	Design and implement best-in-class sales processes, leveraging technology and analytics for optimal outcomes.
	Monitor and evaluate performance metrics, using insights to refine strategies and ensure alignment with business objectives.
	Champion cross-departmental collaboration to drive seamless execution of initiatives.


Team Leadership and Development:


	Lead, mentor, and inspire a high-performing sales team, fostering accountability,innovation, and growth.
	Create a structured environment for team development, equipping members with the skills to excel in dynamic market conditions.
	Cultivate a results-driven culture where ambition and collaboration drive success.


Required Skills and Experience
Microfinance Expertise:


	Minimum of 7 years of experience in sales and leadership roles, with significant time spent in the microfinance banking sector.
	In-depth knowledge of microfinance products, regulatory frameworks, and market dynamics.


Strategic and Organisational Excellence:


	Demonstrated ability to create and implement long-term strategies that yield measurable success.
	Exceptional organisational and process-building skills, ensuring seamless execution and scalability of operations.


Leadership Acumen:


	Proven track record of leading high-performing teams and delivering results in complex environments.
	Strong interpersonal skills to build trust and credibility with internal and external stakeholders.


Sales Prowess:


	Consistent history of exceeding sales targets in competitive markets.
	Expertise in managing client relationships, negotiating high-value deals, and delivering customer-focused solutions.


Who You Are


	Visionary Thinker: You possess a long-term perspective and can connect strategic goals to actionable plans.
	Operational Architect: You are skilled in designing systems and processes that drive efficiency and impact.
	Industry Expert: Your knowledge of the microfinance sector sets you apart as a trusted leader and advisor.
	Results-Oriented Leader: You consistently deliver measurable outcomes and thrive on driving meaningful change.
	Collaborative Mentor: You lead with empathy, inspiring your team to achieve collective success.


Nice to Haves:


	Professional certifications in sales, leadership, or microfinance operations.
	Experience driving digital transformation initiatives in the financial services sector.
	Exposure to executive-level decision-making and strategic planning.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to:&nbsp;thehrhive@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-07",
  "validThrough": "2026-03-19",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

  },

Senior Executive Job at HR Hive




        
    

        
        TheHRHive - Our client, a Financial Services firm, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Executive

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Senior Executive is a pivotal role for an experienced professional with unmatched expertise in the microfinance banking sector.
	This individual will drive high-level sales strategy, lead market expansion efforts, and play a key role in shaping the bank’s growth trajectory.
	The ideal candidate combines a visionary mindset with a structured, data-driven approach,bringing exceptional organisational skills and strategic insight to every facet of the role.
	Beyond achieving sales objectives, this leader will contribute significantly to building operational systems and fostering a high-performance culture, positioning Aella for long-term success in the dynamic financial ecosystem.


Key Responsibilities
Strategic Leadership:


	Architect and execute sales strategies that align with the bank’s vision for market leadership and revenue growth.
	Analyse market trends, customer behaviour, and regulatory developments to inform proactive decision-making.
	Partner with executive leadership to integrate sales objectives with overall organisational goals, offering strategic input on business priorities.


Market Expansion and Business Growth:


	Identify and capitalise on emerging opportunities in untapped markets, ensuring sustainable growth.
	Build relationships with key stakeholders across the microfinance sector, enhancing the bank’s visibility and influence.
	Lead the introduction and scaling of innovative financial solutions tailored to diverse customer needs.


Operational Excellence:


	Design and implement best-in-class sales processes, leveraging technology and analytics for optimal outcomes.
	Monitor and evaluate performance metrics, using insights to refine strategies and ensure alignment with business objectives.
	Champion cross-departmental collaboration to drive seamless execution of initiatives.


Team Leadership and Development:


	Lead, mentor, and inspire a high-performing sales team, fostering accountability,innovation, and growth.
	Create a structured environment for team development, equipping members with the skills to excel in dynamic market conditions.
	Cultivate a results-driven culture where ambition and collaboration drive success.


Required Skills and Experience
Microfinance Expertise:


	Minimum of 7 years of experience in sales and leadership roles, with significant time spent in the microfinance banking sector.
	In-depth knowledge of microfinance products, regulatory frameworks, and market dynamics.


Strategic and Organisational Excellence:


	Demonstrated ability to create and implement long-term strategies that yield measurable success.
	Exceptional organisational and process-building skills, ensuring seamless execution and scalability of operations.


Leadership Acumen:


	Proven track record of leading high-performing teams and delivering results in complex environments.
	Strong interpersonal skills to build trust and credibility with internal and external stakeholders.


Sales Prowess:


	Consistent history of exceeding sales targets in competitive markets.
	Expertise in managing client relationships, negotiating high-value deals, and delivering customer-focused solutions.


Who You Are


	Visionary Thinker: You possess a long-term perspective and can connect strategic goals to actionable plans.
	Operational Architect: You are skilled in designing systems and processes that drive efficiency and impact.
	Industry Expert: Your knowledge of the microfinance sector sets you apart as a trusted leader and advisor.
	Results-Oriented Leader: You consistently deliver measurable outcomes and thrive on driving meaningful change.
	Collaborative Mentor: You lead with empathy, inspiring your team to achieve collective success.


Nice to Haves:


	Professional certifications in sales, leadership, or microfinance operations.
	Experience driving digital transformation initiatives in the financial services sector.
	Exposure to executive-level decision-making and strategic planning.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: thehrhive@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

        TheHRHive - Our client, a Financial Services firm, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Legal Manager

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Overview


	We’re looking for an experienced Legal Managerwith top-tier law firm training and strong fintech or financial services exposure.
	This role will lead legal strategy, manage risk, and ensure regulatory compliance across all areas of the digital bank.


Key Responsibilities

	Manage legal operations across regulatory compliance, contracts, data protection, lending, and employment
	Oversee engagement with external counsel and regulatory bodies (CBN, NDIC, etc.)
	Draft and negotiate commercial agreements and product terms
	Advise on legal risk across all departments, particularly in growth and product.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor`s Degree 
	8+ years of legal experience, including at a top 4 Nigerian law firm and preferably in-house within fintech or banking
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian financial regulation
	Clear communicator and business-minded legal advisor.


Method of Application
